title: Cyclosorus elmerorum
intro: Cyclosorus elmerorum, also known as the Elmer's cyclosorus or Elmer's fern, is a species of fern in the family Thelypteridaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Cyclosorus elmerorum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional herbal preparations, Cyclosorus elmerorum is often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being dried and used in herbal capsules or tinctures.
taxonomy: Cyclosorus elmerorum is classified within the family Thelypteridaceae, a group of ferns commonly known as the marsh ferns or thelypterid ferns. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics and its position in the botanical classification system. The family Thelypteridaceae is part of the order Polypodiales, which includes a wide range of ferns. Cyclosorus elmerorum is further classified as a species within the genus Cyclosorus, a group of Asian ferns. The exact classification of Cyclosorus elmerorum is still a subject of ongoing research and may be subject to revision.
